title: Canon XV.

source.title: ANF06. Fathers of the Third Century: Gregory Thaumaturgus, Dionysius the Great, Julius Africanus, Anatolius, and Minor Writers, Methodius, Arnobius

verses.book: The Canonical Epistle, with the Commentaries of Theodore Balsamon and John Zonaras.
verses.text: No one shall find fault with us for observing the fourth day of the week, and the preparation, on which it is reasonably enjoined us to fast according to the tradition.

verses.book: The Canonical Epistle, with the Commentaries of Theodore Balsamon and John Zonaras.
verses.text: On the fourth day, indeed, because on it the Jews took counsel for the betrayal of the Lord; and on the sixth, because on it He himself suffered for us.

verses.book: The Canonical Epistle, with the Commentaries of Theodore Balsamon and John Zonaras.
verses.text: But the Lord’s day we celebrate as a day of joy, because on it He rose again, on which day we have received it for a custom not even to bow the knee.

verses.book: The Canonical Epistle, with the Commentaries of Theodore Balsamon and John Zonaras.
verses.text: Conformably to the sixty-fourth Apostolical canon, which decrees that we are not to fast on the Sabbath, with one exception, the great Sabbath; and to the sixty-ninth canon, which severely punishes those who do not fast in the Holy Lent, and on every fourth day of the week and day of preparation.

verses.book: The Canonical Epistle, with the Commentaries of Theodore Balsamon and John Zonaras.
verses.text: Thus also does the present canon decree.

verses.book: The Canonical Epistle, with the Commentaries of Theodore Balsamon and John Zonaras.
verses.text: Always, says he, are the fourth and sixth days of every week to be kept as fasts; nor will any one find fault with us for fasting on them; and the reasons he subjoins.

verses.book: The Canonical Epistle, with the Commentaries of Theodore Balsamon and John Zonaras.
verses.text: But on the Lord’s day we ought not to fast, for it is a day of joy for the resurrection of the Lord, and on it, says he, we have received that we ought not even to bow the knee.

verses.book: The Canonical Epistle, with the Commentaries of Theodore Balsamon and John Zonaras.
verses.text: This word, therefore, is to be carefully observed, “we have received,” and “it is enjoined upon us according to the tradition.”

verses.book: The Canonical Epistle, with the Commentaries of Theodore Balsamon and John Zonaras.
verses.text: For from hence it is evident that long-established custom was taken for law.

verses.book: The Canonical Epistle, with the Commentaries of Theodore Balsamon and John Zonaras.
verses.text: Moreover, the great Basil annexes also the causes for which it was forbidden to bend the knee on the Lord’s day, and from the Passover to Pentecost.

verses.book: The Canonical Epistle, with the Commentaries of Theodore Balsamon and John Zonaras.
verses.text: Read also the sixty-sixth and sixty-ninth Apostolical canons.
